text: In some Native languages the term for plants translates to 'those who take care of us.'
Earth laughs in flowers.
One touch of nature makes the whole world kin.
To plant a garden is to believe in tomorrow.
Weeds are flowers too, once you get to know them.
A garden is never so good as it will be next year.
A weed is a plant that has mastered every survival skill except for learning how to grow in rows.
A weed is a plant that is not only in the wrong place but intends to stay.
Never go to a doctor whose office plants have died.
All the flowers of tomorrow are in the seeds of today.
To an optimist every weed is a flower; to a pessimist every flower is a weed.
A garden is a grand teacher. It teaches patience and careful watchfulness; it teaches industry and thrift; above all it teaches entire trust.
Don't wait for someone to bring you flowers. Plant your own garden and decorate your own soul.
Every flower that ever bloomed had to go through a whole lot of dirt to get there.
Flowers are the music of the ground. From earth's lips spoken without sound.
I was just sitting here, enjoying the company. Plants got a lot to say if you take the time to listen.
Plants are solar powered air purifiers whose filter never needs replacing.
The glory of gardening: hands in the dirt, head in the sun, heart with nature. To nurture a garden is to feed not just the body, but the soul.
We may think we are nurturing our garden, but of course it's our garden that is really nurturing us.
A garden requires patient labor and attention. Plants do not grow merely to satisfy ambitions or to fulfill good intentions. They thrive because someone expended effort on them.
May your life be like a wildflower, growing freely in the beauty and joy of each day.
If you have a garden and a library, you have everything you need.
Gardens are not made by singing 'Oh, how beautiful,' and sitting in the shade.
It takes a long time to grow old friends.
